title: The shoeshine boy in Marrakech
summary: A short portrait of a child working in a busy market square.
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a young worker you saw or met in another country. Describe what they did, how they spoke to you, and how it made you feel. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: On a hot afternoon in…, I was sitting at a cafe in the main square when…
He could not have been more than…
He polished my shoes faster than I expected and…
sampleText: On a hot afternoon in Marrakech, I was sitting at a cafe at the edge of Jemaa el-Fnaa with a mint tea in front of me. A boy with a wooden box came up to my table and pointed at my dusty shoes. He could not have been more than ten years old, and he had a serious face that looked older than the rest of him. I nodded, and he immediately knelt down on the dirty stones. He worked very quickly. First he brushed off the dust, then he put a small amount of dark polish on a soft cloth and rubbed it into the leather in slow circles. While he was polishing, he hummed a song to himself. He never asked for a price. When he finished, he looked up and smiled shyly. I gave him twenty dirhams, which was more than the normal tip. He thanked me in three languages, then ran across the square to a group of friends who were waiting near a fruit stall. My shoes looked almost new for the next two weeks. Every time I looked down, I thought about that boy and his small wooden box and his soft humming voice.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I was sitting at a cafe at the edge of Jemaa el-Fnaa
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đang ngồi ở một quán cà phê ở rìa quảng trường Jemaa el-Fnaa

sampleAnnotations.phrase: A boy with a wooden box came up to my table
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Một cậu bé với một cái hộp gỗ đến bàn tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He could not have been more than ten years old
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Cậu chắc chỉ chừng mười tuổi là cùng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a serious face that looked older than the rest of him
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một khuôn mặt nghiêm túc trông già hơn phần còn lại của cậu

sampleAnnotations.phrase: knelt down on the dirty stones
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: quỳ xuống trên những viên đá bẩn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: rubbed it into the leather in slow circles
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chà nó vào da theo những vòng tròn chậm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: While he was polishing, he hummed a song to himself
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Trong khi cậu đang đánh bóng, cậu ngân nga một bài hát một mình
nhiều hơn mức tiền boa thông thường

sampleAnnotations.phrase: looked almost new for the next two weeks
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: trông gần như mới trong hai tuần sau đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Comparative: 'older than the rest of him'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: So sánh hơn với tính từ ngắn dùng -er + than: 'older than the rest of him' = già hơn phần còn lại của cậu. So sánh phải có hai đối tượng được nối bởi than.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Short adjectives form comparatives with -er + than: 'older than the rest of him'. A comparative needs two things connected by than.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple sequence of actions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong kể chuyện, dùng past simple cho chuỗi hành động liên tiếp: 'First he brushed off the dust, then he put a small amount of dark polish on a soft cloth and rubbed it into the leather.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: In narration, use past simple for a sequence of completed actions: 'First he brushed off the dust, then he put a small amount of dark polish on a soft cloth and rubbed it into the leather.'

sampleTranslationVi: Vào một buổi chiều nóng ở Marrakech, tôi đang ngồi ở một quán cà phê ở rìa quảng trường Jemaa el-Fnaa với một ly trà bạc hà trước mặt. Một cậu bé với một cái hộp gỗ đến bàn tôi và chỉ vào đôi giày dính bụi của tôi. Cậu chắc chỉ chừng mười tuổi là cùng, và có một khuôn mặt nghiêm túc trông già hơn phần còn lại của cậu. Tôi gật đầu, và cậu lập tức quỳ xuống trên những viên đá bẩn. Cậu làm việc rất nhanh. Đầu tiên cậu phủi bụi đi, rồi đặt một lượng nhỏ xi đánh giày màu đậm lên một miếng vải mềm và chà nó vào da theo những vòng tròn chậm. Trong khi cậu đang đánh bóng, cậu ngân nga một bài hát một mình. Cậu không bao giờ hỏi giá. Khi xong, cậu ngước lên và mỉm cười e dè. Tôi đưa cậu hai mươi dirham, nhiều hơn mức tiền boa thông thường. Cậu cảm ơn tôi bằng ba ngôn ngữ, rồi chạy qua quảng trường đến một nhóm bạn đang đợi gần một sạp trái cây. Đôi giày tôi trông gần như mới trong hai tuần sau đó. Mỗi lần tôi nhìn xuống, tôi nghĩ về cậu bé đó và cái hộp gỗ nhỏ và giọng ngân nga nhẹ của cậu.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past continuous to set a scene.
options: I was sitting at a cafe at the edge of Jemaa el-Fnaa.
I sat at a cafe at the edge of Jemaa el-Fnaa.
I have been sitting at a cafe at the edge of Jemaa el-Fnaa.
I am sitting at a cafe at the edge of Jemaa el-Fnaa.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous sets the ongoing scene before another event begins.

options: more old than the rest of him
older than the rest of him
old than the rest of him
the oldest than the rest of him
to wash with soap and water
to break into small pieces
to make a surface shiny by rubbing it
to glue something together

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sequence.
options: First he brushed off the dust, then he putted polish on a cloth.
First he was brushing off the dust, then he puts polish on a cloth.
First he brush off the dust, then he was put polish on a cloth.
First he brushed off the dust, then he put polish on a cloth.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple of 'put' is 'put' (no -ed). Both verbs past simple.

options: in a quiet, not-confident way
in a loud, proud way
in a quick, angry way
in a strict, professional way

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the natural sentence with 'never asked for'.
options: He never asked a price for.
He never asked for a price.
He asked never for a price.
Never he asked for a price.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Ask for + noun' = request; 'never' goes between subject and verb.

options: a hint or piece of advice
the end of a pencil
extra money given to a worker for good service
a small bag of food
mcQuestions.explanation: Noun 'tip' in this context = extra money for service.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'rubbed it ___ the leather.'
options: on
to
by
into
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Rub something into a surface' is the natural collocation.

options: he hummed a song to himself
he hum a song to himself
he humming a song to himself
he was humm a song to himself
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'hummed' (one syllable + consonant doubling) is correct.

options: completely broken
nearly like new, very well restored
very old
too small to wear

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural opening for a description of a child worker.
options: He could not has been more than ten years old.
He could not has been more than ten year old.
He could not have been more than ten years old.
He could not be having more than ten years old.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Could not have been more than X' is a fluent way to estimate.

criticalThinkingQuestion: When you give extra money to a child worker, are you helping them or making something difficult easier to ignore? What is the right thing to do?
